{"trigger.triggerid":["add",33645],"trigger":["add"],"trigger.opdata":["add","Current state: {ITEM.LASTVALUE1}"],"trigger.comments":["add","This trigger expression works as follows:
1. It can be triggered if the operations status is down.
2. `{$IFCONTROL:"enp0s3"}=1` - a user can redefine the context macro to "0", marking this interface as not important. No new trigger will be fired if this interface is down.
3. `last(/TEMPLATE_NAME/METRIC,#1)<>last(/TEMPLATE_NAME/METRIC,#2)` - the trigger fires only if the operational status was up to (1) sometime before (so, does not fire for "eternal off" interfaces.)

WARNING: if closed manually - it will not fire again on the next poll, because of .diff."],"trigger.flags":["add",4],"trigger.priority":["add",3],"trigger.recovery_mode":["add",1],"trigger.manual_close":["add",1],"trigger.expression":["add","{$IFCONTROL:"enp0s3"}=1 and {51495}=2 and ({51496}<>{51497})"],"trigger.recovery_expression":["add","{51495}<>2 or {$IFCONTROL:"enp0s3"}=0"],"trigger.tags[22826]":["add"],"trigger.tags[22826].tag":["add","scope"],"trigger.tags[22826].value":["add","availability"]}

{"item.hostid":["add","11459"],"item.templateid":["add","69411"],"item.name":["add","Memory page faults per second"],"item.key_":["add","perf_counter_en[\"\\Memory\\Page Faults\/sec\"]"],"item.description":["add","Page Faults\/sec is the average number of pages faulted per second. It is measured in number of pages faulted per second because only one page is faulted in each fault operation, hence this is also equal to the number of page fault operations. This counter includes both hard faults (those that require disk access) and soft faults (where the faulted page is found elsewhere in physical memory.) Most processors can handle large numbers of soft faults without significant consequence. However, hard faults, which require disk access, can cause significant delays."],"item.delay":["add","1m"],"item.tags[75046]":["add"],"item.tags[75046].tag":["add","component"],"item.tags[75046].value":["add","memory"],"item.tags[75046].itemtagid":["add","75046"],"item.interfaceid":["add","844"],"item.itemid":["add","70385"]}

{"item.hostid":["add","11459"],"item.templateid":["add","69412"],"item.name":["add","Memory pages per second"],"item.key_":["add","perf_counter_en[\"\\Memory\\Pages\/sec\"]"],"item.description":["add","This measures the rate at which pages are read from or written to disk to resolve hard page faults.\r\nIf the value is greater than 1,000, as a result of excessive paging, there may be a memory leak."],"item.delay":["add","1m"],"item.tags[75047]":["add"],"item.tags[75047].tag":["add","component"],"item.tags[75047].value":["add","memory"],"item.tags[75047].itemtagid":["add","75047"],"item.interfaceid":["add","844"],"item.itemid":["add","70386"]}

{"item.hostid":["add","11459"],"item.templateid":["add","69413"],"item.name":["add","Memory pool non-paged"],"item.key_":["add","perf_counter_en[\"\\Memory\\Pool Nonpaged Bytes\"]"],"item.value_type":["add","3"],"item.units":["add","B"],"item.description":["add","This measures the size, in bytes, of the non-paged pool. {"item.hostid":["add","11459"],"item.templateid":["add","69415"],"item.name":["add","CPU DPC time"],"item.key_":["add","perf_counter_en[\"\\Processor Information(_total)\\% DPC Time\"]"],"item.units":["add","%"],"item.description":["add","Processor DPC time is the time that a single processor spent receiving and servicing deferred procedure calls (DPCs). DPCs are interrupts that run at a lower priority than standard interrupts. `% DPC Time` is a component of `% Privileged Time` because DPCs are executed in privileged mode. If a high `% DPC Time` is sustained, there may be a processor bottleneck or an application or hardware related issue that can significantly diminish overall system performance."],"item.delay":["add","1m"],"item.tags[75051]":["add"],"item.tags[75051].tag":["add","component"],"item.tags[75051].value":["add","cpu"],"item.tags[75051].itemtagid":["add","75051"],"item.interfaceid":["add","844"],"item.itemid":["add","70389"]}

{"item.hostid":["add","11459"],"item.templateid":["add","69419"],"item.name":["add","Context switches per second"],"item.key_":["add","perf_counter_en["\System\Context Switches/sec"]"],"item.description":["add","Context Switches/sec is the combined rate at which all processors on the computer are switched from one thread to another.
Context switches occur when a running thread voluntarily relinquishes the processor, is preempted by a higher priority ready thread, or switches between user-mode and privileged (kernel) mode to use an Executive or subsystem service.
It is the sum of Thread\\Context Switches/sec for all threads running on all processors in the computer and is measured in numbers of switches.
There are context switch counters on the System and Thread objects. This counter displays the difference between the values observed in the last two samples, divided by the duration of the sample interval."],"item.delay":["add","1m"],"item.tags[75055]":["add"],"item.tags[75055].tag":["add","component"],"item.tags[75055].value":["add","cpu"],"item.tags[75055].itemtagid":["add","75055"],"item.interfaceid":["add","844"],"item.itemid":["add","70393"]}

value.resourcename: Linux: Interface enp0s3: Ethernet has changed to lower speed than it was before
value.details:
{"trigger.triggerid":["add",33581],"trigger":["add"],"trigger.opdata":["add","Current reported speed: {ITEM.LASTVALUE1}"],"trigger.comments":["add","This Ethernet connection has transitioned down from its known maximum speed. This might be a sign of autonegotiation issues. Acknowledge to close the problem manually."],"trigger.flags":["add",4],"trigger.priority":["add",1],"trigger.recovery_mode":["add",1],"trigger.manual_close":["add",1],"trigger.expression":["add","{51376}<0 and {51377}>0
and
({51378}=6 or {51378}=1)
and
({51379}<>2)"],"trigger.recovery_expression":["add","({51376}>0 and {51380}>0) or
({51379}=2)"],"trigger.dependencies[56259]":["add"],"trigger.dependencies[56259].dependsOnTriggerid":["add",33582],"trigger.tags[22737]":["add"],"trigger.tags[22737].tag":["add","scope"],"trigger.tags[22737].value":["add","performance"]}

{"trigger.triggerid":["add",33582],"trigger":["add"],"trigger.opdata":["add","Current state: {ITEM.LASTVALUE1}"],"trigger.comments":["add","This trigger expression works as follows:
1. It can be triggered if the operations status is down.
2. `{$IFCONTROL:"enp0s3"}=1` - a user can redefine the context macro to "0", marking this interface as not important. No new trigger will be fired if this interface is down.
3. `last(/TEMPLATE_NAME/METRIC,#1)<>last(/TEMPLATE_NAME/METRIC,#2)` - the trigger fires only if the operational status was up to (1) sometime before (so, does not fire for "eternal off" interfaces.)

WARNING: if closed manually - it will not fire again on the next poll, because of .diff."],"trigger.flags":["add",4],"trigger.priority":["add",3],"trigger.recovery_mode":["add",1],"trigger.manual_close":["add",1],"trigger.expression":["add","{$IFCONTROL:"enp0s3"}=1 and {51381}=2 and ({51382}<>{51383})"],"trigger.recovery_expression":["add","{51381}<>2 or {$IFCONTROL:"enp0s3"}=0"],"trigger.tags[22738]":["add"],"trigger.tags[22738].tag":["add","scope"],"trigger.tags[22738].value":["add","availability"]}

It is measured in number of pages faulted per second because only one page is faulted in each fault operation, hence this is also equal to the number of page fault operations. This counter includes both hard faults (those that require disk access) and soft faults (where the faulted page is found elsewhere in physical memory.) Most processors can handle large numbers of soft faults without significant consequence. However, hard faults, which require disk access, can cause significant delays."],"item.delay":["add","1m"],"item.tags[74867]":["add"],"item.tags[74867].tag":["add","component"],"item.tags[74867].value":["add","memory"],"item.tags[74867].itemtagid":["add","74867"],"item.uuid":["add","24b8aae0da0f42808fcf316360ef5268"],"item.itemid":["add","70292"]}

{"item.hostid":["add","11458"],"item.name":["add","Memory pages per second"],"item.key_":["add","perf_counter_en[\"\\Memory\\Pages\/sec\"]"],"item.description":["add","This measures the rate at which pages are read from or written to disk to resolve hard page faults.\r\nIf the value is greater than 1,000, as a result of excessive paging, there may be a memory leak."],"item.delay":["add","1m"],"item.tags[74868]":["add"],"item.tags[74868].tag":["add","component"],"item.tags[74868].value":["add","memory"],"item.tags[74868].itemtagid":["add","74868"],"item.uuid":["add","43a1a73f3c97477586baca00a234a164"],"item.itemid":["add","70293"]}

{"item.hostid":["add","11458"],"item.name":["add","CPU DPC time"],"item.key_":["add","perf_counter_en[\"\\Processor Information(_total)\\% DPC Time\"]"],"item.units":["add","%"],"item.description":["add","Processor DPC time is the time that a single processor spent receiving and servicing deferred procedure calls (DPCs). {"item.hostid":["add","11458"],"item.name":["add","Context switches per second"],"item.key_":["add","perf_counter_en["\System\Context Switches/sec"]"],"item.description":["add","Context Switches/sec is the combined rate at which all processors on the computer are switched from one thread to another.
Context switches occur when a running thread voluntarily relinquishes the processor, is preempted by a higher priority ready thread, or switches between user-mode and privileged (kernel) mode to use an Executive or subsystem service.
It is the sum of Thread\\Context Switches/sec for all threads running on all processors in the computer and is measured in numbers of switches.
There are context switch counters on the System and Thread objects. This counter displays the difference between the values observed in the last two samples, divided by the duration of the sample interval."],"item.delay":["add","1m"],"item.tags[74876]":["add"],"item.tags[74876].tag":["add","component"],"item.tags[74876].value":["add","cpu"],"item.tags[74876].itemtagid":["add","74876"],"item.uuid":["add","403c497fc06841609de0b5d6f02b780d"],"item.itemid":["add","70300"]}

value.details: {"item.itemid":["add",70207],"item.delay":["add","1m"],"item.hostid":["add",11456],"item.name":["add","sda: Disk read request avg waiting time (r_await)"],"item.key":["add","vfs.dev.read.await[sda]"],"item.type":["add",15],"item.units":["add","!ms"],"item.params":["add","(last(//vfs.dev.read.time.rate[sda])/(last(//vfs.dev.read.rate[sda])+(last(//vfs.dev.read.rate[sda])=0)))*1000*(last(//vfs.dev.read.rate[sda]) > 0)"],"item.description":["add","This formula contains two Boolean expressions that evaluate to 1 or 0 in order to set the calculated metric to zero and to avoid the exception - division by zero."],"item.flags":["add",4],"item.tag[74742]":["add"],"item.tag[74742].tag":["add","component"],"item.tag[74742].value":["add","storage"],"item.tag[74743]":["add"],"item.tag[74743].tag":["add","disk"],"item.tag[74743].value":["add","sda"]}

{"trigger.triggerid":["add",33519],"trigger":["add"],"trigger.opdata":["add","Current state: {ITEM.LASTVALUE1}"],"trigger.comments":["add","This trigger expression works as follows:
1. It can be triggered if the operations status is down.
2. `{$IFCONTROL:"enp0s3"}=1` - a user can redefine the context macro to "0", marking this interface as not important. No new trigger will be fired if this interface is down.
3. `last(/TEMPLATE_NAME/METRIC,#1)<>last(/TEMPLATE_NAME/METRIC,#2)` - the trigger fires only if the operational status was up to (1) sometime before (so, does not fire for "eternal off" interfaces.)

WARNING: if closed manually - it will not fire again on the next poll, because of .diff."],"trigger.flags":["add",4],"trigger.priority":["add",3],"trigger.recovery_mode":["add",1],"trigger.manual_close":["add",1],"trigger.expression":["add","{$IFCONTROL:"enp0s3"}=1 and {51267}=2 and ({51268}<>{51269})"],"trigger.recovery_expression":["add","{51267}<>2 or {$IFCONTROL:"enp0s3"}=0"],"trigger.tags[22650]":["add"],"trigger.tags[22650].tag":["add","scope"],"trigger.tags[22650].value":["add","availability"]}

{"trigger.triggerid":["add",33479],"trigger":["add"],"trigger.opdata":["add","Current state: {ITEM.LASTVALUE1}"],"trigger.comments":["add","This trigger expression works as follows:
1. It can be triggered if the operations status is down.
2. `{$IFCONTROL:"enp0s3"}=1` - a user can redefine the context macro to "0", marking this interface as not important. No new trigger will be fired if this interface is down.
3. `last(/TEMPLATE_NAME/METRIC,#1)<>last(/TEMPLATE_NAME/METRIC,#2)` - the trigger fires only if the operational status was up to (1) sometime before (so, does not fire for "eternal off" interfaces.)

WARNING: if closed manually - it will not fire again on the next poll, because of .diff."],"trigger.flags":["add",4],"trigger.priority":["add",3],"trigger.recovery_mode":["add",1],"trigger.manual_close":["add",1],"trigger.expression":["add","{$IFCONTROL:"enp0s3"}=1 and {51190}=2 and ({51191}<>{51192})"],"trigger.recovery_expression":["add","{51190}<>2 or {$IFCONTROL:"enp0s3"}=0"],"trigger.tags[22588]":["add"],"trigger.tags[22588].tag":["add","scope"],"trigger.tags[22588].value":["add","availability"]}

{"trigger.triggerid":["add",33439],"trigger":["add"],"trigger.opdata":["add","Current state: {ITEM.LASTVALUE1}"],"trigger.comments":["add","This trigger expression works as follows:
1. It can be triggered if the operations status is down.
2. `{$IFCONTROL:"enp0s3"}=1` - a user can redefine the context macro to "0", marking this interface as not important. No new trigger will be fired if this interface is down.
3. `last(/TEMPLATE_NAME/METRIC,#1)<>last(/TEMPLATE_NAME/METRIC,#2)` - the trigger fires only if the operational status was up to (1) sometime before (so, does not fire for "eternal off" interfaces.)

WARNING: if closed manually - it will not fire again on the next poll, because of .diff."],"trigger.flags":["add",4],"trigger.priority":["add",3],"trigger.recovery_mode":["add",1],"trigger.manual_close":["add",1],"trigger.expression":["add","{$IFCONTROL:"enp0s3"}=1 and {51113}=2 and ({51114}<>{51115})"],"trigger.recovery_expression":["add","{51113}<>2 or {$IFCONTROL:"enp0s3"}=0"],"trigger.tags[22526]":["add"],"trigger.tags[22526].tag":["add","scope"],"trigger.tags[22526].value":["add","availability"]}
